rationale: Each PR spawning an ephemeral RunPod (H100 or A100) shifts spend from the per-launch budget to the per-merge budget. Until P2 contract freeze (roadmap entry #74) clears CP1 we hold this behind a manual cost gate.

Ephemeral pods are useful primarily for verifying the live LoRA serving path; the CPU-only smoke layer (anima_serve_smoke + ci_serve_alm_persona_smoke) already exercises contract schemas without GPU. Activating preview env before CP1 = paying GPU cost for low-yield validation.

must_have: weekly H100 spend < $200 sustained for 2 weeks

runpod_template.rationale: A6000 is the cheapest 48GB tier ($0.49/hr) — sufficient for Qwen-14B inference without quant. We do NOT spawn H100 for previews; H100 is reserved for actual training runs (P2 + CP1).

spawn: 6. expose port via runpod proxy URL
7. POST {pr_number, preview_url, ttl} to GitHub PR comment via gh CLI
validate: 2. POST /v1/chat/completions with smoke prompt → expect non-empty echo
teardown_trigger: PR closed (any close: merged or rejected)
manual: gh workflow run pr_preview_teardown.yml -f pr=<num>
any push that triggers a new spawn (replace, not stack)
teardown: 3. POST teardown notice to PR comment

security.secrets_source: github actions secrets only — never commit to repo
security.no_persistent_volume: ephemeral pods only — no state survives teardown
outbound_allowlist: api.openai.com (for /v1 compat shim parity tests only)

raw_strict: raw#9 raw#10 raw#11 raw#15 spec_only deterministic LLM=none
